channel.title: Posts on Jonas Bučinskas
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Jonas Bučinskas

item.title: What to expect from Founder.University?
item.description:
What to expect from Founder.University?

My co-founder Ignas and I just wrapped up a 12-week Founder.University pre-accelerator programme. Putting this blog post in the books primarily for ourselves, so we could share it out the next time someone asks us “Should I join Founder Uni?”.

Hint: You should!

What is Founder.University?

Founder University (FU for short) will teach you how to start a technology company. It’s a 12-week pre-accelerator for startups that is run by Kelly and Presh, and is part of a larger LAUNCH venture investment firm/accelerator headed by none other than Jason Calacanis himself.

item.title: README / User Manual
item.description:
README / User Manual

Interested in creating your own user manual? Check out this article

Last update: Apr 7, 2026

About

Usually, I revisit this document once a year. Always surprised to see how my approach to product, company and now people development has evolved. Every year I learn something new and in a lot of cases those learnings are about things that I should stop doing or pay attention to. Sometimes, though, I might decide to include an entirely new section.

item.title: Kaunas Has the Audacity
item.description:
Kaunas Has the Audacity

A few years ago I was about an hour away from boarding a flight to New York when a stranger in front of me just plainly asked “What’s happening in Kaunas in 2022?”. Honestly, it threw me off a little until I realized I was wearing a T-Shirt with “Kaunas 2022” written all over it. After quickly gathering my thoughts, I launched into a pitch.

I talked about what the European Capital of Culture is, how every year a few new cities take the title, what it means, and how Kaunas is going to be different. I have closely followed the preparations, checked who’s behind all of it, attended a couple of early gatherings(that’s how I got the T-Shirt), and had a feeling that they were cooking something really special.

item.title: Building products the right way
item.description:
I love experimentation, iteration, creating proofs of concept. I, also, strongly believe that any code that is not in production is completely useless. That being said, as a person who ships software products for a living, the #1 struggle I have is launching something that is “good enough”.

Product launch that is “good enough” really means that:

“We just launched something that meets some baseline expectations of our potential buyers who use similar products already”.
